project.name: Peria
project.tagline: Human-readable by default. LLM-ready by design.
project.description: Peria turns codebase knowledge into a traceable technical wiki.
project.audience: Engineers and AI coding agents who need reliable codebase context.
project.problem: Codebases lose practical knowledge when architecture, commands, docs, and Git history live in separate places.
project.currentFocus: Build a local-first self-documenting wiki pipeline before adding hosted integrations.
highlights: Markdown wiki pages are the canonical artifact.
llms.txt is a compact reading map derived from the human wiki.
Every generated claim should trace back to source files, line numbers, and Git context.

pages.title: Peria Overview
pages.description: Peria turns codebase knowledge into a traceable technical wiki.

pages.title: Packages
pages.description: Workspace packages, manifests, scripts, dependencies, and package exports.

pages.title: CLI Commands
pages.description: Registered Peria CLI commands and their command registry provenance.

pages.title: TypeScript Modules
pages.description: Source files, imports, and exported declarations extracted with ts-morph.

pages.title: Adapters
pages.description: Framework adapters that expose the generated docs in application runtimes.

pages.title: Configuration And Features
pages.description: Resolved config, enabled features, and config provenance.

pages.title: Wiki UI
pages.description: Static visual wiki shell generated into /docs and backed by markdown pages.

pages.title: History
pages.description: Recent Git history captured as dated provenance for the generated wiki.

pages.title: AI Context Map
pages.description: A compact reading map for agents, derived from the human wiki.
